title: PSG-Arsenal: Paris braces for a tense weekend with Champions League final and several large concerts
subhed: 'This is an exceptional situation that calls for an exceptional operation,' said a source within Paris's police prefecture, which is planning to deploy an additional 8,000 officers to secure the Parc des Princes football stadium and an Aya Nakamura concert.

title: The Bolloré protégée spreading Russian propaganda in France

Chancellor Friedrich Merz's unpopularity fuels talk of finding a replacement
subhed: One year after taking office, Germany's head of federal government has reached record levels of unpopularity, his coalition is riven by internal disagreements and the economy is shaken. The far right's score in three regional elections in September could put him to the test.

title: US hails 'productive' talks between Israel and Lebanon
subhed: Friday's meeting between Israeli and Lebanese military officials was the first in decades. The Pentagon's second-in-command said on X that the 'productive military-to-military discussions' will 'inform the Department of State-led political track next week' and added that the US 'anticipates reconvening soon to continue the security track.'

title: 'Latte dads': The not-so-new Scandinavian fathers
subhed: Sweden is promoting a new masculine ideal: the caring, attentive father sipping matcha on a café terrace beside a stroller. But is this the sign of a real shift, or simply a reinvention of the 'new father' stereotype? So far, statistics have yet to confirm these parental feats.

title: The oil crisis suits TotalEnergies' CEO perfectly
subhed: Between price caps at the pump and controversies over windfall profits, Patrick Pouyané is once again at the center of public debate. Despite the tensions, he dreams of proving that the multinational company serves the French people's interest.

title: Iran is gradually reconnecting to the internet, though access remains limited
subhed: After a three-month blackout, Iran regained partial internet access on Tuesday, May 26. The Intelligence Ministry has warned Iranians against collaborating with any foreign media, which it accused of espionage.

title: 38° at 11 am. French students and staff struggle with unbearable classroom temperatures
subhed: Across France, primary, middle and high schools have reported the severity of the situation in their overheated classrooms. Education professionals are criticizing the lack of response in recent years from both local authorities, who are responsible for school buildings, and from the Education Ministry, in charge of the safety of students and staff.
